000 — About

Who I Am

I'm a Mechanical Engineering graduate from Murang'a University of Technology, currently working as a Graduate Engineer Intern at KASP3R Technologies; where I operate laser cutting, laser welding, and bending machines, and help design and fabricate mechanical systems like brake press assemblies.

Outside the workshop, I build things on screen too: CAD models in SOLIDWORKS, structural checks through Finite Element Analysis, and the occasional web or graphic design project. I also write poetry; a different kind of precision, but precision all the same.

I like work that moves from sketch to something you can actually touch, click, or read.

002 — Project Experience

Built & Tested

FIG. 01 / CAD

CAD Modelling — SOLIDWORKS

3D models built for production use, including a mesh-front storage cabinet and a mobile service cart assembly; taken from concept sketch to dimensioned, manufacturable parts.

FIG. 02 / CNC

CNC Laser Cutting — KASP3R Internship

Hands-on laser cutting, laser welding, and bending work during my internship, producing both functional parts and decorative panel designs.

FIG. 03 / HYDRAULICS

Hydraulic Lift Model

Designed and built a small hydraulic lift to demonstrate fluid power and mechanical control principles — translating theory into a working physical mechanism.

FIG. 04 / AUTOMATION

Aquatic Waste Collection System

Developed a prototype for collecting waste from water bodies using a motorized mechanism with simple automation, aimed at low-cost environmental cleanup.
